name: Canonicalisation
def: Converting input to a single, canonical form before validating or authorising it, so equivalent encodings cannot bypass security checks.
problem: The same value has many representations (URL-encoding, Unicode, path traversal, mixed case); validating the raw form lets attackers smuggle malicious input past filters.
mechanism: Decode and normalise first — resolve percent/Unicode encodings, collapse path traversals (../), normalise Unicode (NFC), unify case/separators — then validate and authorise the single canonical result. Reject anything that does not canonicalise cleanly.
limits: Order matters: must canonicalise before checks
whenNot: n/a — always canonicalise untrusted input before security decisions
myths: "Blocklisting bad patterns is enough" — without canonicalisation, encoded variants slip through
